metadata.note: Section 210A of MV Amendment Act 2019 allows states to apply multiplier of 1x to 10x on central fines. Some states have chosen specific fixed amounts instead.

Delhi.notes: Delhi uses specific fixed fines, often higher than central. ANPR cameras active at 800+ junctions.
additional_rules: Odd-even scheme applicable on weekdays 8AM-10AM and 5PM-9PM in winter
No-parking zones strictly enforced with towing
BS-VI vehicles mandatory for new registrations

Maharashtra.notes: Maharashtra reduced some fines from central Act after protests. Mumbai and Pune have separate traffic police.
additional_rules: Helmets mandatory for pillion riders
Triple riding on two-wheelers prohibited
Mumbai: Lane driving strictly enforced on expressways

Tamil Nadu.notes: Tamil Nadu follows central fine amounts largely. CCTV enforcement in Chennai and Coimbatore.
additional_rules: PUCC (Pollution Under Control Certificate) mandatory - checked at borders
Autorickshaws must use meters in Chennai
Night driving restrictions for heavy vehicles in city limits

Karnataka.notes: Bengaluru has active AI-powered ANPR camera enforcement. Challan sent digitally to registered mobile.
additional_rules: Bengaluru: Riding without helmet on IT parks roads also enforced
E-challan sent via SMS to registered mobile number
Speed limits: 50 kmph in city, 80 kmph on state highways, 100 kmph on national highways

Kerala.notes: Kerala initially did not implement full MV Act 2019 fines. Gradual adoption ongoing.
additional_rules: Strict enforcement during festive seasons (Onam, Vishu)
School zone speed limit: 30 kmph strictly enforced

Gujarat.notes: Gujarat took time to implement MV 2019 fines. Currently partially implemented.
additional_rules: Dry state - no alcohol - DUI rarely reported but strictly penalized
Ahmedabad BRTS lanes: strict enforcement for private vehicles entering

Rajasthan.notes: Follows central Act fines. Enforcement active on national highways (NH48, NH52).
additional_rules: Desert highway speed limits strictly enforced - 100 kmph on NH
Night heavy vehicle restrictions in Jaipur city

Uttar Pradesh.notes: UP has active echallan system. Lucknow and Noida have camera-based enforcement.
additional_rules: Expressway speed limit: 120 kmph for cars
Heavy vehicles banned from Agra-Lucknow expressway during peak hours

West Bengal.notes: Kolkata has active traffic police. State partially adopted MV 2019 fines.
additional_rules: Kolkata: Yellow taxis regulated by West Bengal Transport Corporation
Tram zone speed limits in Kolkata: 15 kmph

Telangana.notes: Hyderabad has one of the best ANPR enforcement systems in India.
additional_rules: Hyderabad: Drunk driving crackdowns every weekend
ORR (Outer Ring Road): 100kmph speed limit strictly enforced

Haryana.notes: Follows central fine schedule. Active enforcement on NH48 (Delhi-Mumbai corridor).
additional_rules: NH48: Speed cameras every 20km
Gurugram: Strict action on e-rickshaws violating routes
